---
title: "Page header"
---
# Page header

The page header identifies the current page and surfaces its primary actions. It appears at the top of every main content area.

***

## When to use

- At the top of every main page to orient the user and provide primary actions.
- Do not use inside modals, panels, or nested sections. Those use their own title patterns.

## Style

- The title should match the name of the item or section exactly.
- Use sentence case for page titles.
- Keep action labels in the header short. Prefer one or two words. For example, "New dataset" not "Create a new dataset".
- Avoid putting more than two primary actions in the header. Move secondary actions to a menu.

## Do / Don't

| Do | Don't |
|---|---|
| New connection | Create a new connection |
| Edit | Edit this item |
| Sales pipeline | Sales Pipeline Overview |
